title: Matrix BFS Traversal (Up, Right, Down, Left)
prompt:
Given a 2D array (matrix), return the values visited by a breadth-first search starting at the top-left cell (0, 0).

Traversal rules:
- Visit a cell, then explore neighbors in this order: up, right, down, left.
- Do not visit a cell more than once.
- Stay within bounds.

Return an array of visited values in the order they are first visited.
If the matrix is empty, return an empty array.
constraintQuestions: What if the matrix is empty? (Return []).
Should we visit each cell at most once? (Yes.)
What directions are allowed? (Up, right, down, left only.)
Does traversal order matter? (Yes, use the given direction order.)

walkthrough.title: BFS with queue
walkthrough.body:
Use a queue of coordinates.
Mark visited when a cell is dequeued to avoid repeats.
Enqueue neighbors in the given order.
Collect values as you visit cells.

codeSnippets.title: JavaScript
C#
Python
codeSnippets.body:
from collections import deque


def bfsTraversal(matrix):
    if not matrix or not matrix[0]:
        return []

    rows, cols = len(matrix), len(matrix[0])
    seen = [[False] * cols for _ in range(rows)]
    q = deque([(0, 0)])
    seen[0][0] = True
    result = []
    dirs = [(1, 0), (-1, 0), (0, 1), (0, -1)]

    while q:
        r, c = q.popleft()
        result.append(matrix[r][c])

        for dr, dc in dirs:
            nr, nc = r + dr, c + dc
            if 0 <= nr < rows and 0 <= nc < cols and not seen[nr][nc]:
                seen[nr][nc] = True
                q.append((nr, nc))

    return result

walkthrough.title: Complexity
walkthrough.body:
Time: O(r*c) because each cell is visited once.
Space: O(r*c) for the visited grid and queue.
